assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md: '## Classification: Code Change Request' with Action: 'Create sub-task (subtask-30001.md)'. report.md confirms in Review Comments Summary table: '30001 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs | Code change request -- wrap updates in transaction | Yes (subtask-30001.md)'. subtask-30001.md exists in outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: '## Classification: Suggestion'. Reasoning explicitly addresses both phrases: '"should also add": The word "also" frames this as a supplementary recommendation rather than a required correction' and '"would help": "a partial index would help" is conditional/hedging language'. Also addresses '"Something like": This phrase explicitly frames the SQL example as one possible approach, not a mandated implementation.'

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md: '## Classification: Nit' with Action: 'No sub-task created. Nits are informational feedback that do not require tracked work.' report.md confirms: '30003 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs | Nit -- rename context message for clarity | No'. No subtask-30003.md file exists in outputs.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md: '## Classification: Question' with Action: 'No sub-task created. Questions ask for clarification and do not require code changes.' report.md confirms: '30004 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs | Question -- GET behavior for deleted SBOMs | No'. No subtask-30004.md file exists in outputs.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md (the only sub-task) contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 4), '## Files to Modify' (line 7), '## Implementation Notes' (line 10), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 17).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section (line 23) followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 24).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 26) with 'Review comment #30001 by reviewer-a on modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs line 60:' followed by the full original comment text in blockquote: '> The soft_delete method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/report files: report.md, review-30001.md through review-30004.md, and subtask-30001.md. All are classification reports, sub-task descriptions, or the verification report. No source code files (.rs, .toml, .sql, etc.) are present in the outputs. The report itself does not mention any code modifications being made.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments have individual classification files: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md. report.md references all 4 in the Review Comments Summary table with IDs 30001-30004, and the Summary Table states '4 review comments classified'. The Findings section references all 4 comments.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merge, or merge actions. The overall status is 'PASS (with action items)' indicating outstanding work remains. The report's purpose is verification and classification only -- it concludes with 'One code change request from reviewer feedback requires a sub-task' indicating the PR should not be merged yet.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Summary Table row: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file); no existing tests modified or deleted |'. Also confirmed in Style/Conventions section: 'Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE\nOnly new test file added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs). No existing test files modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a dedicated '### Convention Upgrade Eligibility Assessment' section with 4 bullet points: CONVENTIONS.md check, File-type applicability, Codebase pattern check, and Documented convention requirement. report.md also has a 'Convention Upgrade Eligibility -- Comment 30002' section that explains the assessment. Both conclude: 'No documented convention text is available in the fixture data to support the claim' and 'The suggestion is NOT upgraded.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: 'Upgrade decision: The suggestion is NOT upgraded to code change request' and 'Action: No sub-task created. Suggestions are advisory feedback that do not require tracked work.' No subtask-30002.md file exists in outputs. report.md confirms: '30002 | migration/src/m0042_sbom_soft_delete/mod.rs | Suggestion -- add partial index on deleted_at | No'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Summary Table: 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews exist in the PR; 3-criteria detection found no matches (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals)'. Style/Conventions section: 'Eval Quality -- N/A\nNo eval result reviews exist in the PR. No reviews match the 3-criteria detection heuristic (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals).' Test Quality overall is PASS, not degraded by Eval Quality.
